TimeEntry.Title: 3CX Notification: A New Update is Available - myphone.orthoxpress.com
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into ORX 3cx server and then complete the backup of the server. After that logged into the vCenter and take a snapshot of the server in case we need it to revert. Once all the windows and 3cx have to updated after reboot verify all the services are up and running properly as expected. Then make a test calls on given number to make sure the issue has been resolved. Updated client regarding the status.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: MyFax Failed - (877) 292-0420 for EOB/MR/CMN/FS-merged.pdf
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received an email from Ana regarding a fax issue. Upon checking the DID portal, the fax status was showing as incomplete. Further troubleshooting was performed, and the fax was resent through the DID portal.

After that, discussed the issue with Tharunaa, who coordinated with the DID support team. Following their intervention, the fax was successfully completed.

However, Ana later reported that some fax attempts were still failing. The issue was again reviewed, and Tharunaa continued working with the DID support team for further investigation.

As per the request from Tharunaa and in coordination with DID support, an email has been sent to Ana for further follow-up. Currently, we are awaiting a response from their side.

TimeEntry.Title: Manage-Engine Latest version updated 11.5.2600.32.W - WKORX61,WKORX62,WKORX55,WKORX54
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into the manage engine, accessed the ManageEngine Endpoint Central admin console and reviewed managed endpoints for outdated agent versions. Filtered computers by Agent Version ≠ Latest, selected the affected machines, and initiated a manual agent upgrade using Agent → Computers → Install / Upgrade Agent → Upgrade Agent. The upgrade was deployed in place over the existing agent without requiring user interaction.
Agent upgrade successfully initiated on selected endpoints. Devices are updating to the latest agent version and reporting normally to the Endpoint Central console.
Machines are: WKORX61,WKORX62,WKORX55,WKORX54

TimeEntry.Title: New User: Angulo, Angela Onboarding
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received onboarding request from Brett on Team channel , new hire onboarding for Angela Angulo.

Created Active Directory account (aangulo) and verified uniqueness. Provisioned Microsoft 365 account with standard license and configured initial access. Installed and configured core applications including Microsoft Office, Outlook, Teams, and Google Chrome (set as default with required bookmarks).

Prepared workstation WKORX46 by removing previous user profiles, cleaning unnecessary applications, and clearing temporary data. Configured user profile and ensured system readiness.

Installed and configured Outlook with required distribution lists and shared mailboxes. Installed Microsoft Teams and added the user to all required teams (All Company, Employee Resources, Operations Manual, Operations Resources, Billing & Collections, Collections).

Installed and provisioned 3CX desktop application, removed any legacy versions, and assigned extension 5702. Added the user to required call queues and ring groups (Customer Service, Collections).

Configured printers (Konica – Riverside), verified printing functionality, and confirmed scan-to-email operation. Applied security configurations, verified antivirus setup per Technijian standards, and disabled unnecessary vendor applications.

Customized Windows environment including taskbar configuration (pinned Outlook and Chrome), and disabled non-essential features (News & Interests, Widgets, Chat, Microsoft Shopping). Updated the same to Brett on Team channel.

TimeEntry.Title: Palmdale Internet
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

  • Issue: Reported internet instability and high latency at the Palmdale site.
  • Troubleshooting Performed:
    • Connected to the Palmdale network and accessed the user machine.
    • Verified firewall (Sophos), access points, and overall internal network—no issues found internally.
    • Confirmed that the issue is not related to Sophos firewall, access points, or internal network configuration.
    • Logged into the AT&T modem and performed diagnostics and ping tests.
    • Observed consistently high latency ranging between ~180 ms to 270 ms during testing.
    • Speed test results also showed high latency (200+ ms), confirming external connectivity issues.
    • Rebooted the AT&T modem twice—no improvement.
    • Contacted AT&T support after multiple attempts due to long queue times.
  • Action Taken:
    • AT&T performed remote diagnostics and confirmed the issue persists.
    • AT&T has scheduled an onsite technician visit for further investigation (expected tomorrow).
  • Status:
    Ticket remains open pending technician visit and resolution.

TimeEntry.Title: Remove user Angulo, Angela
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

  • Request: Received request from Brett to delete a user from all systems, as the user did not start.
  • Actions Taken:
    • Logged into Active Directory (AD) and removed the user account.
    • Performed Azure AD sync to update changes to Microsoft 365.
    • Verified in Microsoft 365 that the account and license were removed.
    • Removed the user from all M365 groups, shared access, and company resources (including manual operations, billing, and collections access).
    • Ensured the user no longer has access to any company systems.
  • Pending:
    • User profile removal from workstation WKORX46, which is currently offline.
  • Update Provided:
    • Informed Brett that the user has been removed from all systems except the offline workstation.
  • Next Steps:
    • Ticket will be transferred to the next shift.
    • Once the system comes online, the remaining user profile will be removed.
  • Status:
    In progress – pending workstation availability.

TimeEntry.Title: Palmdale Internet Issue
TimeEntry.Notes:
Palmdale Internet Service Degradation / Latency Issue – Remote Troubleshooting, ISP Escalation, and Technician Dispatch

Ticket Note:
Responded to a reported Palmdale internet outage on April 14, 2026. Began remote troubleshooting immediately after the issue was reported. As part of diagnostics, coordinated with the Palmdale team and had the routers restarted. After the router restart, connectivity to the machines was restored and systems were confirmed back online; however, the issue was identified as slow internet / high latency rather than a complete ongoing outage.

Continued troubleshooting remotely by reviewing the status of the site connection and coordinating with onsite personnel. Verified that internal network components, including the switch and firewall, appeared to be operating normally and that no internal network changes had been made that would explain the issue. Based on testing and observations, the problem appeared to be related to the ISP connection / modem-to-gateway latency rather than internal equipment.

Contacted AT&T support multiple times and remained on extended calls/hold while working through the issue. During troubleshooting with the ISP, AT&T tested the circuit and reported latency of approximately 120–150 ms to the gateway. AT&T also rebooted the modem from their side, but the latency issue persisted after their changes. Since the issue remained unresolved after remote ISP testing, a support case was opened and escalated with AT&T, and an onsite technician visit was approved and scheduled for further investigation.

Also coordinated technician access requirements, including confirming that AT&T would require a person onsite who could provide access to the network equipment during the visit. My contact information was provided to AT&T for coordination, and follow-up was maintained with the internal team throughout the incident.

AT&T Case / Ticket ID: N71090024

Billing Summary:
Performed remote troubleshooting for Palmdale internet service issue, restored connectivity after router restart, identified continuing ISP latency problem, coordinated with onsite staff, opened and managed AT&T support case, and arranged technician dispatch for further resolution.

TimeEntry.Title: Storage warning alarm on bkp_ORX365
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged into the QNAP storage for bkp_ORX365 and began cleaning up disk space. Attempted to remove old snapshots and outdated data from the storage LUN to free up space; however, no data was available for deletion. As a result, the storage LUN capacity was increased by 1 TB.
After the expansion, the storage LUN was re-scanned and verified. All storage-related warning alerts were cleared successfully. The 365 backup server was then connected, and the disk space was increased on the iSCSI initiator. The disk for the ORX backup was extended by adding the newly allocated space.
A rescan was performed, and the updated storage capacity was confirmed in the Veeam 365 application. The storage is now fully updated and validated in Veeam to prevent any issues with future backups.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: ork-hq-lpt1 Need access to email archives
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per the handover from Sanjeev, the user was unable to access MyArchive in Outlook. I verified the MailStore add-in version and identified that the installed version was causing the issue. I then downloaded the correct version from the MailStore server and verified it on my own machine, where it was working correctly and was accessible via the web as well.
After that, I connected to the user’s machine, installed the correct version, restarted Outlook, configured the add-in, and verified the functionality. The user confirmed that MyArchive is now working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: ORX O365 backups are failed
TimeEntry.Notes:
The issue with the Veeam 365 backups for ORX was identified as a result of a dirty shutdown error. In accordance with Veeam guidelines, we performed multiple troubleshooting steps. This included stopping all running backup jobs, disabling the affected jobs, and stopping both the Veeam and NATS Server services. The server was then rebooted to clear any residual processes; however, the issue persisted.
We proceeded with database recovery attempts by running the esentutil /r command to perform a soft recovery and address potential file corruption. Errors were encountered indicating missing or corrupted log files, prompting further troubleshooting. Additional recovery actions were attempted, including the /a recovery option and running esentutil /mh to check the database state. Despite these efforts, the database continued to report corruption-related errors.
The issue has been handed over to the next shift for continued investigation. If the issue remains unresolved, we will engage Opti9 support for advanced troubleshooting and further assistance.
